initial_program:
% Determine grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- N = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- true.
col(0..M-1) :- true.

% Identify rows that contain a 2 and count them
row_has_two(R) :- input(R,C,2).

count_two_rows = #count{ R : row_has_two(R) }.

top_two_row  = #min{ R : row_has_two(R) }.
bottom_two_row = #max{ R : row_has_two(R) }.

% For the single2 case we also need the column of the 2 in the topmost row.
% Choose a column that contains the 2 in that row (there will be at least one).
single_two_row = top_two_row.
single_two_col = 0 :- (count_two_rows = 1, row_has_two(R), R = top_two_row, 
                         #count{ C : input(R, C, 2) } = 1).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Helper predicates for the leftmost nonzero cell in a row
% (C is the first column where input is nonzero)
leftmost(R,C,V) :- row(R), col(C), input(R,C,V),
                   (C = 0 ; forall D < C => not input(R,D,_)).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Default copy  every cell keeps its original colour
output(R,C,Col) :- input(R,C,Col).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Transform rows when there are multiple 2rows (count_two_rows > 1)
% In that case only rows with a 2 in the same row or the row below are changed.
% For those rows we fill left of the first nonzero with its colour.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
constraints_single :-
    count_two_rows = 1,
    R = top_two_row,
    (forall D < top_two_row => not input(R,D,_) ;
     C = 0 ; row(R), col(C), input(R,C,2)) ;
    % Transform rows r where r < R (rows above the 2row)
    R2 = 0,
    R2 <= R-1,
    leftmost(R,Rc,Val) :- row(R2), col(C), input(R2,C,Val),
                         (C = 0 ; forall D < C => not input(R2,D,_)).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% Constraints that enforce the pattern for rows above the single 2
% (rows r < top_two_row)
constraints_single_rows :-
    R = top_two_row,
    L = top_two_col,
    (R - 2 >= 0) ; leftcol = L - 2 ; leftcol = 0 ;  % safe bound

    % For each row r < R:
    r = 0,
    r <= R-1,
    % pattern columns:
    (leftcol = leftcol),
    output(r, leftcol, 3) :- leftcol <= M-1,
    output(r, leftcol+1, 3) :- leftcol+1 <= M-1,
    output(r, R, 2) :- R = C2,
    output(r, c, 3) :- leftcol+2 <= c, c < M.

constraints_multiple :-
    count_two_rows > 1,
    (row_has_two(R0), R0 = top_two_row),
    (row_has_two(R0), R0 = bottom_two_row),
    % Transform rows r where r  [top_two_row-1 , bottom_two_row]
    R0 = 0,
    (R0 >= top_two_row-1 ; R0 <= bottom_two_row),
    leftmost(R0,C0,Val) :- row(R0), col(C0), input(R0,C0,Val),
                        (C0 = 0 ; forall D < C0 => not input(R0,D,_)).

    % For each such row, fill left side with the leftmost colour
    output(R0, C, Val) :- row(R0), col(C), C <= C0.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Final output is exactly the one defined above (no extra shows)
#show output/3.
